IBEW.text:
**IBEW Traveler Snapshot — Tuesday, July 7, 2026**

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** — Port Washington Data Center is eating. Four contractors (Gurtz, MJ Electric, Cupertino, Uihlein) all posting the same gig: **JW wage plus $10/hr** on top, 50–60 hours weekly, drug test mandatory. That's a hard premium in a tight labor market. Book depth sits around 174 hands for 38 calls — not a glut. If you can pass screening and swallow the grind, this one pays.

**LU-237 Niagara Falls, NY** — Lake Mariner Data Center is the anchor. Ferguson and O'Connell both hiring **$48.20/hr** with per diem available, 7/10s first shift. Only 101 hands on the board for 7 calls means you've got real leverage here. Orientation runs Mon/Tue; move fast.

**LU-430 Racine, WI** — Microsoft Mount Pleasant is still rolling. Four contractors (Gerdes, Pieper, Staff, Kelso Burnett) all posting inside wireman slots at **$62.51/hr**, Wisconsin reciprocal state license accepted. 76 hands, 11 calls. Long sleeves, conduit work, no drama.

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** — Meta and Google data centers dominate. Meade Electric's Meta gig in Lebanon pays **$53.80/hr** (10% above scale), 5–12s M–F plus Saturdays, new construction. Industrial Electric's Eli Lilly rotation is 7–10s on a 13-day rule. 180 hands, 36 calls—still available but tightening.

**LU-683 Columbus, OH** — Cologix Data Center (Lewis Center) via ESI: **$54.50/hr** top scale, all OT double time, drug test. Mid City and TSG have scattered inside work. 115 hands, 24 calls. Solid backup if you're in the Midwest corridor.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** — Polaris Forge Data Center near Harwood is the outlier: **$44.80/hr** but **$100/day non-taxed per diem** plus shift differentials and weekend bonuses ($150). Parsons Electric owns the board. 100+ mile GPS radius applies. Book's tight (108 hands, 24 calls) but the lodging arithmetic changes the math.

Data centers have consolidated the entire board—seven of your top ten locals are chasing DC work. Hands are following the infrastructure spend.

LINEMAN.text:
**IBEW Lineman Snapshot — Tuesday, July 7, 2026**

**LU-1547 Anchorage, AK** — 44 calls chasing 59 hands on the board, but book depth is tight. Golden Valley Electric Association is running **$30.86/hr** temporary laborer shifts (10–12 hrs, week-on/week-off) at the Healy Powerhouse, while Alaska Communications posts Journeyman I&R gigs at **$45.31/hr** in Anchorage proper. Wolf Creek Federal Services has **$55.84/hr** Fire Alarm slots at Ft. Greely if you hold NICET II and a DoD Secret clearance. Scale runs to $91K-ish annual; the play here is patience and credentials.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** — 10 calls, 95 hands sitting, but this is solid ground. Summit Line and Cupertino Electric both posting **$55.00/hr** Other work with OT double time; Rosendin's running **$40 + $15 incentive** (hitting 90% of 60-hr weeks). All three main contractors (Summit, Cupertino, B&D) require NM State JIW License EE98J. Book is deep but stable.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** — 24 calls, ~108 hands needed. Parsons Electric is the heavy hitter: 28 + 20 + 14 Inside JW slots on the Polaris Forge Data Center (Harwood) at **$44.80/hr**, 6–10s, with **$100/day non-taxed per diem** if you're 100+ miles out; weekday incentives hit $100/day, Sat/Sun jumps to $150/day. Hunt Electric adds 10 slots at the Grand Forks Air Base, same **$44.80** scale, 4–10s with Friday OT. This is *work volume* — 72 hands across three contractors on two projects.

**LU-291 Boise, ID** — 13 calls, 36 hands. VECA Electric leads with 10 Inside JW slots at **$65.33/hr** (non-PLA, META KND 3&5 project, incentive OT premium). Micron PLA work sits lower (**$45.33/hr** + **$170/day per diem x7** if eligible, Travel Pay $20/hr). Book is moderately open.

**LU-474 Memphis, TN** — 3 calls but 47 hands stacked thick. ArchKey Sachs Electric has 40 Inside JW slots on the Google Data Center (West Memphis, AR) at **$36.65/hr**, 6am start, 4–10s/2–8s with **$125/day incentive**. American Electric and McCrory rounding out with rigid conduit and traffic signal work.

**LU-66 Houston, TX** — 10 calls, 23 hands (lean board). NHPL's transmission work (Floresville) pays **$175/day per diem** for Large Operators (Class A CDL + NCCCO); Front Line's Overhead Distro runs JY Lineman roles, no per diem listed. This is feeder work, not stack.

**Data center build-outs—Polaris, Google, META—are siphoning hands across the map; transmission and industrial maintenance still moving steady, but the real chase right now is inside wireman on infrastructure mega-projects.**
